target.name: Anysphere
target.founder: Michael Truell, Sualeh Asif, Aman Sanger, Arvid Lunnemark

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $7.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $300B+

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $183B

private.name: Neuralink
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $9.7B

private.name: Groq
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $7B

data.narrative:
Anysphere is a private-stage AI & Software company currently valued at $29.3B, having raised $3.2B+ to date. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with primary private peers including OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Neuralink ($9.7B), and Groq ($7.0B).

A peer-median approach applied to the four most directly comparable private AI & Software firms yields a valuation of $96.3B. This cohort spans a wide range—from Groq at $7.0B to OpenAI at $300.0B—reflecting significant heterogeneity within frontier AI. The median of $96.3B anchors our analysis given limited applicability of traditional revenue or margin multiples at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $81.9B (low) to $110.8B (high), centered on $96.3B. This implies Anysphere trades at a discount of approximately 67% to the peer median, suggesting either a valuation discount relative to OpenAI and Anthropic or, alternatively, a more conservative market assessment of the company's current stage and risk profile. Key uncertainties include: heterogeneity in peer company maturity and revenue generation; limited public comparables in the AI infrastructure space; and the substantial gap between the current valuation ($29.3B) and the peer-derived range, which warrants examination of company-specific circumstances not captured in peer metrics alone.
